item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DID SOMEBODY SAY SHARK?! Time for my fist to enter the face of an unspecified aquatic organism.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Posting here for clarity.

As per the information above, considering none of these explicitly state that the pictures are licensed under CC 3.0, they are assumed not compliant and thus have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well the marine biologist in me is a happy fellow at this and the use of a wobbegong essentially forces me to upvote this. It's friggin vote extortion is what it is! +1

item.title: Image sources
item.content:encoded:
top pic

appears to be taken from an article at Gamme.com.tw about an abandoned aquarium. I don't read Chinese so I can't be more specific, but there's no indication that this is Creative Commons.

https://news.gamme.com.tw/53808

middle pic

"Aquarium", a 2007 artwork by Lori Nix, of Blood Milk Jewelry. Her stuff is super cool, but is copyrighted; that said, she might be willing to let it be used here, if asked.

https://www.klatmagazine.com/photography/lori-nix-the-power-of-nature-pics-066/17372

bottom pic is from a 2010 article on Vice, exploring a dead Japanese aquarium called "Navel Land" (yes, they misspelled "Naval"). No indication that image is Creative Commons.

https://www.vice.com/en_ca/article/qbzgp3/more-abandoned-japanese-stuff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS is the shark punching center? I'm getting more of a "sanctuary for post apocalypse refugees" Vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sometimes people type SPC instead of SCP.. This article has a literal center for shark punching that apparently came from an alternate universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone explain to me what the Shark punching center is? I'm REALLY confused and I don't know why it's tagged in this article

Okay. Now that I read it again the TAE links make sense. Which can only mean one thing…

Activate the Head Cannon!
- Fiiiiiirrrreeee!

The Third Antarctic Empire and the Shark Punching Center inhabit the same alternate Earth. It's a small multiverse after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes almost too much sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I just realized that this scip and author commentary thereupon implies that the serious!SPC may exist in the same universe as the Third Antarctic Empire. I think I headcanon this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THE GOOD SHIT
i particularly like the "pregnant" woman evocative of those seabirds and fish whose bellies are full of trash.

item.title: And I shall call it...
item.content:encoded: Bioshock.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want a game based off this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…terrifying/warm-cuddly images of Dr. Wondertainment breeding and selling terrestrial carpet sharks as pets.

Dammit, now I want a carpet shark plushie. =o[

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If removed, it is not replaced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this SCP and the ways that it links to SCP-1449 and to the SPC (in a nicely serious manner).
One question: It says:

Stable phenomena are present at all times

I assume that these phenomena can be removed, given the analysis of the coeleocanth meat. When removed, are they replaced, or is this indicating that the phenomena continue to exist even if removed from the site?

If they can be removed and are just replaced, that just gives me terrifying/warm-cuddly images of Dr. Wondertainment breeding and selling terrestrial carpet sharks as pets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Opening the door to room B106 will reveal an SCP-1329-1 subject (SCP-1329-1-28) in the specimen tank, being attacked by a juvenile Carcharhinus leucas (Bull shark) The subject will struggle for approximately thirty seconds, beating at the shark’s head and attempting to gouge out its eyes, before dying.

And this was the man who wanted to join the Shark Punching Center. Bah!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoa! This hit 100 while I wasn't looking!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How strange that both of my comments this evening happen to be on things you wrote…

Anyway, this ALSO thoroughly amused me. The Tank of Random Coelacanth Meat, The Brain Jellies, The Infamous Floor Wobbegong (Which is what won me over.), The Truck Shark, The Great Dual Flood. (all of the former would make excellent names for bands…) All of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this in every way. Take my 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like a fractal! A fracticle?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have you read Habibi by Craig Thompson? He illustrates massive amounts of floating trash amazingly well.

I know that's an odd endorsement, but it's a beautiful book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image file is 500 x 323 pixels, but the article shrinks it down to 300 pixels wide. You should shrink down the image file to 300 pixels wide before you upload it, so it downloads quicker.

The tentacle consists of fibrous tissue: closer inspection reveals these fibers to be smaller tentacles.

Heh. It's tentacles all the way down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) I would hope so, because this isn't about the time loops specifically.

2) Mmmm..nope. No experiment log. Never use 'em. Use your imagination.

3) What.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the wildness, but time-stops and ghost stories usually don't do it for me. Reminds me of the stable time loop article, which is much crisper.

Still, it's a good to have creepy things with lots of unanswered questions. I think this calls for experiments.

Also, herring with sour cream and onions. Can't figure out whether to abstain or upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bit about -1 and observers seems to contradict itself. Reading back, I can see that the looping ones don't notice anything outside the loop while one-offs react as expected; but that distinction wasn't at all clear on the first reading.

That is my one nitpick on an otherwise excellent bit. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this immensely-I wish I was able to articulate it better, but like Doctor Ether, my brain is in overdrive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can settle for fists on you eyes. Then you can walk on your hands, letting you use 4 fists to fend off the wobbegong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The world needs more Djoric. We need to set up the infrastructure for Factory-produced Djoric to be shipped out to the masses. Or at the least, refine some concentrated Djoricate to be sent with MCF relief packages to areas heavily afflicted with Djoric deficiency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … and, according to eyewitnesses, the 'carpet became shark'…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If only I had fists on my ankles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"We've got the new carpet installed, boss."

"And? How's it look?"

"Well, you see…it's a shark. The carpet is a shark. A wobbegong, to be precise."

"So the shark is a carpet."

"Yes. It's a carpet shark."

"Tell 'em to watch their ankles and just leave it in there."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is one of the scariest pictures I've seen here, which is no mean feat. Open canoe in the middle of the Pacific?? /shiver

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for wobbegongs.

This. So much this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for wobbegongs.

And also because this is an extremely interesting and thought-provoking article which reads like the author has a seriously over-active imagination, which is easily the best type of article.

But I'd probably upvote any article with a wobbegong in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NOW IT ALL MAKES SENSE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, that bit also ties back to the overall nautical theme. I present to you the Great Pacific Garbage Patch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yo dawg, I heard you like tentacles…

Man. What a charming collection of seriously trippy shit. The somewhat nautical theme helps tie it all together, yet makes things like the pregnant woman and her child stand out even more. I have less than no idea what's going on in this place, but I'm okay with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, you make-a me blush.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the sheer amount of thinking my brain is currently doing. You're one of my favorite authors on here. Keep it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's basically that if they're in a regular, repeated loop of behavior, they won't deviate from that unless someone forces them to. All the others just don't really seem to be completely aware that they're walking around an abandoned aquarium, or that they seem to be a universe away, and are generally unfazed by the people in lab coats watching them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed it anyway for accuracy's sake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was kind of muddy on what you were aiming for with most of this, especially things like the mechanics of the -1 instances noticing observers. That said, the end-note gelled the concept for me nicely. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been awhile since Linguistics 101, but … if it's indeed a pidgin, they're probably not going to get much beyond sub- and superstrate languages—not much more than what you've got in the article, in other words. If it's indeed a pidgin, that does say a lot about the language's age though (in technical usage, a pidgin that's been around long enough for people to teach it to their kids as a mother tongue is called a "creole"), which in turn lets you make some interesting inferences about the kind of society SCP-1329-1 have/had.

That said, my main thought was "hang on, aren't most Central Asian languages really closely related? You could just say 'Turkic', couldn't you?" Which is one of those style niggles that's only going to bother someone who knows the field.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what linguists might conclude about the origins of the pidgin language of SCP-1329-1 from analyzing sufficient samples.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's on your floor Scantron.

It's on the floor and looking at your ankles and your ankles are so tasty looking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh God I just looked that animal up.

Aaaargh

Aaarrrggghhhh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A specimen of Eucrossorhinus dasypogon (Tasseled wobbegong) inhabiting the main office. Specimen will attack any intruding organism at floor level.

If I wasn't going to upvote this because of the generalized awesome or the hints of post-apocalypticness in the note at the end, I would have had to for the carpet shark.

No complaints found. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will neither confirm nor deny this theory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind blowing thought: These are interdimensional manifestations of the "savages and their rotting lands" from the Third Antarctic Empire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In a way. They , at least to me, feel extremely believable. The detailed procedure is outsourced into a document those needing to enact it will get, the manner in which it needs to be guarded is stated cleanly without overspecifying, and overall it just fits the object like a glove.
I'd like to see a lot more containment procedures written in this spirit, rather than "3x3x2.5 room with reinforced concrete walls which is probably a Foundation standard cell, but I'm going to specify it anyways to bulk it up".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed.

Also, I have no idea why you'd like the containment procedures so much. There's not a whole lot to them. Unless that's why you like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wonderful - pretty much one of my favourite types of objects, ones that have something completely WTF going on in a way that hints at some purpose.
Also, I loved the containment procedures.

One thing though

heavily pregnant

Is not clinical tone, it's not even english. Late-term pregnant , if anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This is really intriguing. I love the atmosphere.

Nothing else to add. Take your +1, and spend it all in one place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked this one, well written; upvote.

I tried so very hard not to laugh at that picture, but the funny seems to be a function of time. Damn it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in chat, like it here. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was possessed by a fit of manic creativity late last night.

I regret nothing. NOTHING.

e: Also, I told Roget I would post this picture for him.
